Turn 63 ‚ÄĒ row-action audit before freezing the appendix The right next move is not another obstruction layer. Turn 62 already organized the proof as a certificate contract: fixed orientation, H1 -Nielsen matrix, primitive-boundary verifier, residual induction, nonlinear certificates D.1‚ÄďD.4, coefficient Koszul‚ÄďWitt induction, and finite filtered Nielsen compactness. The problem remains exactly the over-G isomorphism question for the quotients defined from F=P‚čäG and relators rS,rH.The one thing that should be cleaned before the appendix is frozen is row-action notation. The proof uses right őõ=Z2 [[G]]-module relation rows throughout. If a matrix is accidentally printed transposed, two of the key displayed identities become visually ambiguous. This is presentation-level, but important: it is the kind of small notation error that would make a correct proof look false.

1. Fix the row convention explicitly

In the appendix, state:All¬†relation¬†rows¬†act¬†on¬†the¬†right.

Thus if w=(a,b) andU=(u00u10u01u11),then wU=(au00+bu10,au01+bu11).

With this convention, the dangerous Ef-piece H1 -matrix must be printed asU0=( s‚ąí1s‚ąí212).Then (s+3,‚ąí2)U0=((s+3)(s‚ąí1)‚ąí2(s‚ąí2),(s+3)‚ąí4)=(s2+1,s‚ąí1),anddetU0=2(s‚ąí1)‚ąí(s‚ąí2)=s,which is a unit. This is the fixed first Magnus layer used by the filtered Nielsen data; the history records this U-matrix as the H1 start for the compactness construction.

2. Fix the coefficient normalization shear

On the local Ef-coefficient ring

R=Efőõ‚ČÉZ2 [[t]],t=s‚ąí1,the row before normalization is(s2+1,s‚ąí1).

The exact identity is(s2+1)‚ąí(s+1)(s‚ąí1)=2.

Since rows act on the right, the correct elementary shear isN=(1‚ąí(s+1)01).Then(s2+1,s‚ąí1)N=((s2+1)‚ąí(s+1)(s‚ąí1),s‚ąí1)=(2,t).This exact normalization is what turns the local coefficient problem into the regular Koszul row (2,t). It is not merely a congruence modulo (2,t)2; it is an honest Nielsen coordinate change. The coefficient proof should therefore be written only after this normalization. The history‚Äôs Koszul‚ÄďWitt block depends on precisely this reduction to (2,t).

3. Appendix theorem, with the row convention inserted

A clean final appendix theorem should read:Certificate theorem. Assume the following finite certificates are provided.H1 -Nielsen certificate. With ŌĀS=rS‚ąí1, the abelianized rowswS=(ŌÉ+3E,‚ąí2),wH=(Eg0+3E‚ąí2,ŌÉ‚ąíE)are related by some U‚ąąGL2 (őõ), with the Ef-piece printed asU0=( s‚ąí1s‚ąí212).Primitive-boundary certificate. The reduced-coproduct verifier constructs Ln=Primn(F2‚ü®A,B‚ü©), the maps‚ąād:Ld‚äēLd‚ÜíLd+1,(u,v)‚Ü¶[A,u]+[B,v],and the secondary maps őłd0, reproducing the recorded primitive dimensions and ranks. Turn 52 records that the deterministic linear verifier reproduced the primitive dimensions, boundary ranks, and secondary quotient ranks.Residual induction certificate. In the residual equationS(Y0,Y1,q)=(Y1,Y1q)‚ąí1Y1‚ąí2Y03Y0q=X2,the first nonzero error is primitive, odd layers are ordinary boundaries, and even layers reduce to square quotients. The induction Cm=Lm for m‚Č•5 closes the residual tower. Turn 62 records this as the residual contract.Nonlinear certificates D.1‚ÄďD.4. These are the finite exceptional blocks before the uniform inductions take over: D.1 gives six independent degree-10 Bockstein shadows, D.2 gives the residual degree-16 lower-base őĽ-toggle, D.3 is the stack-rank certificate rankD5,6,7=60,rank(D5,6,7őľ4B4)=61,and D.4 is the quotient witness őł80(0,B8)=(B8)[2]. Turn 54 records that the D.3 verifier computed the A2=R/(4,t) coefficient row and verified the rank jump 60‚Üí61.Coefficient Koszul‚ÄďWitt certificate. After the exact row normalization(s2+1,s‚ąí1)‚ąľ(2,t),the first t-layer is contractible, the first 2-Bockstein secondary maps satisfyBd‚Ü†Hd(2)(d‚Č•4),and higher (2,t)-adic layers are killed by the normalized Koszul‚ÄďWitt cascade, ordered by Magnus root degree first. The history records the repaired Koszul‚ÄďWitt mechanism as the coefficient tower closure.Filtered Nielsen compactness certificate. For each coefficient-and-degree truncation (N,J), define finite filtered Nielsen/cocycle solution sets SN,J. The certificates above prove SN,JÓÄ†=‚ąÖ. Since these sets are finite and functorial under refinement, compactness gives a compatible inverse-limit datum, hence a continuous over-G automorphism ő¶:F‚ÜíF withő¶(rS‚ąí1)=rH.Then ő¶(‚ü®‚ü®rS‚ü©‚ü©F)=‚ü®‚ü®rH‚ü©‚ü©F,so ő¶ descends toőďS‚ČÖGőďH.The compactness descent is recorded in the history: finite filtered Nielsen/cocycle data induce finite over-G automorphisms, and their compatible inverse limit yields the desired over-G automorphism of F.

4. What Turn 63 changes

Turn 63 does not add a new mathematical ingredient. It prevents a notation trap in the final write-up. The appendix should explicitly declare row-vector right action and print the two matrices asU0=( s‚ąí1s‚ąí212),N=(1‚ąí(s+1)01).With those conventions fixed, the certificate contract from Turn 62 is manuscript-ready: no obstruction layer remains to be tested; the remaining task is attaching the verifier scripts or explicit matrices/pivots so that the recorded ranks and witnesses are externally reproducible.

ESTABLISHED: The final appendix must use right row-action notation. With that convention, the correct Ef-piece H1 -matrix is U0=( s‚ąí1s‚ąí212), and the correct coefficient-normalizing shear is N=(1‚ąí(s+1)01). These give (s+3,‚ąí2)U0=(s2+1,s‚ąí1) and (s2+1,s‚ąí1)N=(2,t). The proof still concludes őďS‚ČÖGőďH.

GAP: No structural mathematical gap remains inside the attached sweep. The remaining gap is external reproducibility: the standalone appendix should attach the verifier scripts or explicit matrices/pivots, especially for the D.3 stack-rank 60‚Üí61.

NEXT: Insert the row-action convention and the corrected matrix displays into the final appendix, then package the verifier outputs under Certificates A‚ÄďF rather than reopening any new special case.[TURN63-ROW-ACTION-AUDIT-CERTIFICATE-CONTRACT]
